title: Economics of Marine Biology

jokes.text: Sat score zero. And he was recently arrested for selling marijuana to a police officer at a police station.
jokes.setup: Dean lists increasingly bad credentials for a potential student
jokes.punchline: The marijuana arrest escalates to the absurdly specific location
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure with specific absurd detail. Economy is excellent - each word builds the ridiculousness.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: We have a very diverse student body. We accept applicants with all kinds of backgrounds. A marijuana arrest? That's fine. A marijuana arrest in a Whole Foods parking lot? Even better. A marijuana arrest in a Whole Foods parking lot during a organic produce sale? Now you're Greendale material.

jokes.text: Family net worth, $8 million. Ladies and gentlemen, this is what we in the community college recruiting game call a 'whale.'
jokes.setup: After listing terrible credentials, Dean reveals a fortune cookie
jokes.punchline: The twist that money makes him desirable, plus 'community college recruiting game' specificity
jokes.explanation: Solid reversal with perfect character voice. Dean's over-professional terminology for sketchy behavior is very Community.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: It says 'You will be desired by many.' Well, that's not happening anytime soon.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. It also says 'A windfall of money is coming your way.' Oh! Suddenly I'm very desirable!

jokes.text: If you don't like the crispy-licious taste of Let's, feel free to eat that other greasy brand. Splingles? Not this guy.
jokes.setup: Reference to Dean's endorsement deal
jokes.punchline: Dean's awkward corporate speak and made-up chip brands
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor with absurd product names. Dean's corporate enthusiasm is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: I've been approached by several chip companies who want me to be their spokesperson. Now, I take my endorsements very seriously. That's why I'm proud to announce my partnership with Crunchosity™ and Chipulse™ — two of America's most trusted snack brands.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Dean, those aren't real chip brands.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Not yet, Troy. Not yet. But with my Greendale credibility behind them, we're looking at a multi-million dollar enterprise. I've already shot the commercial. It's very tasteful.

jokes.text: Pierce cannot know about this. He's a big baby who throws tantrums when anyone else gets attention.
jokes.setup: Dean explains Pierce has been their whale all along
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of Pierce as 'big baby'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character joke. The bluntness works for Dean's occasionally honest moments.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Pierce has been our whale all along.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: He's just a big baby.

jokes.text: He is the only Greendale student who has ever paid for a premium locker or the extended pencil warranty.
jokes.setup: Describing Pierce as the perfect whale example
jokes.punchline: The incredibly specific and ridiculous upselling that Pierce fell for
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Greendale details. 'Extended pencil warranty' is hilariously specific corporate exploitation.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Pierce is the perfect example of a whale — you know, a high-value customer who will spend enormous amounts of money on the slightest upsell.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: He once paid $300 extra for a coffee maker that brews at exactly 147 degrees because the salesman convinced him that 148 degrees 'releases the sadness in the beans.'

jokes.text: Remember when Kerri Strug visited our campus? - That was horrible. - That was awful.
jokes.setup: Dean references past Pierce tantrum
jokes.punchline: Group's unified traumatic reaction
jokes.explanation: Works through shared traumatic memory. The dual reaction adds emphasis.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Remember when Pierce threw a fit about the air conditioning being too cold?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We don't talk about that.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That was a dark time.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I still have nightmares.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Clinically speaking, we all exhibit symptoms of PTSD.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I can't even look at a thermostat anymore.

jokes.text: I'm in. Sounds like a romper.
jokes.setup: Dean asks for help with recruiting
jokes.punchline: Troy's childlike enthusiasm and made-up word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy line - childlike enthusiasm with invented slang. Economy is excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Troy, I need your help recruiting new students. You're so popular and enthusiastic.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Ooh, Dean! That sounds amazinger! I'll be like a human magnet of studentiality!

jokes.text: The biology department's been dissecting the same dead pig for 10 years.
jokes.setup: Discussion of needing money for equipment
jokes.punchline: Horrifically unsanitary detail delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Greendale's poverty. The specific detail makes it memorably gross.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: We need money for equipment. I could sell my blood plasma, but last time they said I had to stop coming back because my blood kept making the other donors sick. They said it was like mixing Kool-Aid with actual dirt.

jokes.text: I made room in my trophy case. Then, I remembered classes don't give out trophies, so I put a fishbowl there instead.
jokes.setup: Troy mentions making room in trophy case
jokes.punchline: Realizes his mistake, random fishbowl solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy logic - earnest excitement followed by childlike pivot. Character integration is spot-on.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I need to make room in my trophy case for all my awards.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait... I don't have a trophy case.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'll just put them in this fishbowl.

jokes.text: Actually, you can count me in on this one. What? You had me at 'no Pierce.'
jokes.setup: Jeff unexpectedly volunteers after being called out
jokes.punchline: Reveals his petty motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff moment - acts altruistic but reveals selfish motive. Good economy and character truth.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Fine, I'll volunteer.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: But only because someone implied I wouldn't.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: And now I have to prove I'm not selfish by doing something I don't want to do, which is the most selfish thing possible.

jokes.text: You just want to be a part of something. Admit it, Thanksgiving softened that Winger underbelly.
jokes.setup: Jeff's unexpected participation
jokes.punchline: 'Winger underbelly' as anatomical metaphor for emotions
jokes.explanation: Britta's pseudo-psychological insight with vivid imagery. 'Winger underbelly' is quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I have a Winger underbelly.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That's not a real thing.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's the soft, vulnerable part of me that only comes out when I'm with the group.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Or it could just be that you're emotionally stunted and we're enabling it.

jokes.text: They develop nimble fingers from pleasing their mistresses.
jokes.setup: Pierce recommends Italian barbers
jokes.punchline: Casually racist and sexual explanation
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce offensive humor. Character-accurate but relies on shock value rather than cleverness.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You want a real barber? You gotta go to an Italian barber. They know how to treat hair.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Plus, they'll flirt with you the whole time. Makes you feel like a man.

jokes.text: Thank you for opening my eyes to two new stereotypes.
jokes.setup: Pierce's offensive comment
jokes.punchline: Jeff's deadpan acknowledgment of the multiple stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jeff comeback - sardonic, intelligent, and economical. Perfect straight-man response.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's impressive. I don't think I've ever seen someone offend that many people at once. That's like a hate crime buffet.

jokes.text: Walking Dead has left the building. Resume operation, Deadliest Catch.
jokes.setup: Pierce leaves the area
jokes.punchline: TV show puns for their whale hunting mission
jokes.explanation: Clever dual reference connecting Pierce to zombie show and their whale hunt to fishing show.

jokes.text: Hookers, blow, hookers.
jokes.setup: School board suggests improvements to tour
jokes.punchline: Crude escalation with repetition for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Low-effort shock humor. The repetition adds emphasis but it's still crude without cleverness.

jokes.text: College dean threatening a fraternity. You'll never shut down the Delta Cubes.
jokes.setup: Dean rejects fraternity idea
jokes.punchline: Abed immediately embodies college movie trope with absurd frat name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - instantly recognizes and performs genre trope. 'Delta Cubes' is brilliantly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: So what you're saying is we need to start a fraternity.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: No, that's the opposite of what I said.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Right, right. So Delta Tau Chi Lambda.

jokes.text: Physical education education? I thought that was a typo in the course cartalogue.
jokes.setup: Coach introduces PEE class
jokes.punchline: Annie notices the redundancy and misspelled catalog
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with Annie's attention to detail. The misspelling adds to Greendale's incompetence theme.

dialogue.speaker: Coach
dialogue.line: Welcome to Physical Education and Enrichment, or PEE class.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, isn't that redundant? And it's misspelled in the catalog.

jokes.text: A gym is a building, a man-made structure inside which physical education teachers teach physical education to physical education students.
jokes.setup: Troy calls him a gym teacher
jokes.punchline: Overly pedantic correction with circular logic
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific pedantry. The repetition of 'physical education' creates comedic rhythm.

dialogue.speaker: Coach Chapman
dialogue.line: I'm not a gym teacher, I'm a PE instructor. There's a difference.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What's the difference?

dialogue.speaker: Coach Chapman
dialogue.line: A gym teacher teaches in a gym. A PE instructor teaches physical education. I teach physical education.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: In a gym.

dialogue.speaker: Coach Chapman
dialogue.line: Exactly. So I'm a PE instructor.

jokes.text: When my kids talk past bedtime, I use my angry voice, and I say, 'the next peep I hear will be the last.' And I give 'em one of these.
jokes.setup: Coach asks about handling disruptive students
jokes.punchline: Shirley's maternal discipline techniques applied to college students
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley moment - maternal authority with slightly threatening undertone. Character integration is excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Coach
dialogue.line: How do you handle disruptive students in your class?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Well, I use the same techniques I use with my children. A firm hand, clear boundaries, and when necessary, a wooden spoon.

dialogue.speaker: Coach
dialogue.line: You hit college students with a wooden spoon?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Only the ones who talk back. And it works! You should see how fast they sit down and apologize.

jokes.text: The Delta Cubes will never die. We've only just begun to fight. You only began yesterday.
jokes.setup: Abed continues fraternity persona
jokes.punchline: Dean's matter-of-fact deflation of the dramatic statement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the running gag with Dean's practical deflation providing perfect counterpoint.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm not just a member of this fraternity. I'm a way of life. I'm a philosophy. I'm basically the human embodiment of what it means to be Greek.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: You joined last week.

jokes.text: Suck it, bozos. You're getting ditched by the cool kids.
jokes.setup: Jeff pretends he and Pierce have plans
jokes.punchline: Pierce's childish gloating about being 'cool'
jokes.explanation: Pierce's delusional self-image perfectly displayed. The irony of him thinking he's the cool one works well.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Actually, Pierce and I have plans tonight.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: We do? Well, I guess the cool kids are hanging out together.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That's right, I'm cool now. Very cool. The coolest.

jokes.text: Welcome to Greendale. I'm bean Pelton. 'Bean Pelton?' Dean Pelton. Gosh, I'm nervous.
jokes.setup: Dean greets important prospect
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces own title, corrects himself, admits nervousness
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean nervousness leading to mistake. Character-accurate cringe moment that feels earned.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Welcome to Greendale Community College, where we take education very seriously. I'm Dean of Admissions— I mean, Dean of Students. Sorry, I'm a little nervous. You're a very important prospect.

jokes.text: City college bought you a vespa? Brand-new. So, Archie, have you heard about our half-pipes and hash pipes class, taught by professor Shaun White?
jokes.setup: Learning City College gave expensive gift
jokes.punchline: Dean's desperate improvised counter-offer with drug reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Dean's desperation. The hash pipes reference shows how far he'll compromise his values.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Before you decide, let me sweeten the pot. We'll give you a full scholarship, plus a lifetime supply of... of... Adderall.

jokes.text: So, Archie, have you heard about our half-pipes and hash pipes class, taught by professor Shaun White?
jokes.setup: Dean desperately trying to compete with City College
jokes.punchline: Immediately inventing fake drug-related class with celebrity teacher
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Dean's desperation through increasingly absurd promises

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: We need to do something bold to compete with City College. Something that will make Greendale the talk of the town.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I've got it! We'll offer a groundbreaking new course: 'Introduction to Methamphetamine Production: A Culinary Perspective,' taught by celebrity chef Walter White!

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: No wait, he's not available. How about 'Advanced Cocaine Economics' with a hologram of Tony Montana?

jokes.text: My friends and I can drop it off an overpass. Ah, I shouldn't have heard that.
jokes.setup: Dean offers Archie a computer to use
jokes.punchline: Archie casually mentions destructive plan, Dean's horrified realization
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Dean's immediate regret. Shows the whale's destructive nature and Dean's growing awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: You can use my computer anytime you need to.

dialogue.speaker: Archie
dialogue.line: Thanks, Dean. I'm going to use it to plan the destruction of Greendale.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Wait, what?

jokes.text: Well, city college slipped that slope when they slipped him that scooter, but they sure slipped up when they let him set foot on our soil.
jokes.setup: Britta warns about slippery slope
jokes.punchline: Dean's overwrought wordplay with 'slip' variations
jokes.explanation: Dean's tendency toward elaborate wordplay. Slightly overwrought but character-appropriate linguistic showing off.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You're talking about a slippery slope.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Well, I prefer to think of it as a slippery slope that's also a slip 'n' slide, which is both a literal and figurative slip, not to mention the slip of the tongue I just made by saying 'slip' too many times, which is slipping into linguistic redundancy.

jokes.text: You have the spatial reasoning skills of a young Kevin Miller, a really good PE teacher I know.
jokes.setup: Coach complimenting Troy's equipment storage
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific comparison to some random PE teacher
jokes.explanation: Funny specificity in the coach's compliment. The random name-drop feels very coach-like.

dialogue.speaker: Coach
dialogue.line: You know, Troy, your equipment storage is really something special. Reminds me of Coach Harrison down at Lincoln High – he had the same system back in '94.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Coach
dialogue.line: Yeah. Same organization, same attention to detail. He also got arrested for embezzlement.

jokes.text: There's something about a breast that's never been touched by white hands.
jokes.setup: Pierce mentions National Geographic magazines
jokes.punchline: Racist and sexual comment about tribal imagery
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce offensive humor. Character-accurate but relies primarily on shock value over cleverness.

jokes.text: Who teaches bikini class. Oh, yeah! Whoa, check out that bling! I want that.
jokes.setup: Dean's increasingly desperate fake offerings
jokes.punchline: Archie's immediate covetousness of Dean's outfit piece
jokes.explanation: Shows both characters' personalities - Dean's desperation and Archie's materialistic instant gratification.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I'm offering you my ascot!

dialogue.speaker: Archie
dialogue.line: I want it.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: My cardigan!

dialogue.speaker: Archie
dialogue.line: Give it to me.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: My shoes!

dialogue.speaker: Archie
dialogue.line: I need those shoes.

jokes.text: Delta Cubes! Prepare to bond through embarrassment. Pacifiers in!
jokes.setup: Abed continuing his fraternity bit
jokes.punchline: The combination of the lame name with actual hazing involving pacifiers
jokes.explanation: Escalates the Delta Cubes gag with actual hazing activities. The pacifiers are appropriately juvenile.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The combination of the lame name with actual hazing involving pacifiers

jokes.text: No! Line drawn! If I wanted to wait on a rich man hand and foot, I would've gone to Dubai with that sheik I met at Trader Joe's.
jokes.setup: Britta refuses to help serve Archie
jokes.punchline: Specific backstory about rejecting wealthy suitor in mundane location
jokes.explanation: Excellent Britta moment - principled stand with absurd personal anecdote. The Trader Joe's detail makes it perfectly Britta.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I'm not serving Archie. He's a wealthy suitor and I have principles.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Back in high school, a rich guy asked me out at the grocery store produce section and I turned him down. I've never looked back.

jokes.text: I think all a man needs in life is a close shave, a cold beer, and you know, maybe a faithful dog.
jokes.setup: Jeff getting into the barbershop experience
jokes.punchline: The clichéd masculine ideal delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff getting caught up in the moment. The cliché is intentional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: This is it. This is what being a man is about. The barbershop. The camaraderie. The straight razor work.

dialogue.speaker: Barber
dialogue.line: You want the fade?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I want whatever makes me look like I've done something with my life.

jokes.text: I'd look out the window, and there he was, dropping a steaming fat one on the lawn.
jokes.setup: Pierce shares sweet memory of his dog Walter
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation with crude bathroom humor
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce misdirection from sweet to crude. Structure works well even if the humor is lowbrow.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know, I had this dog Walter when I was a kid. He was the sweetest thing. One time he ate a whole wheel of cheese, and then he just sat there looking at me with those big puppy dog eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Next thing I know, he let out the most ungodly fart you've ever heard. Cleared out the whole living room. My mother thought the septic tank had exploded.

jokes.text: This state-of-the-art mock locker room, or 'mocker room,' simulates the conditions of an actual locker room.
jokes.setup: Coach showing training facility
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily complex terminology for fake locker room
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing coach's pretentious approach to simple concepts

jokes.text: Keep yo hands to yourself! Running will get you five laps, mister! I will come in that shower and kick yo bare ass!
jokes.setup: Troy demonstrates PE teaching in mock scenario
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top aggressive coaching with unintentionally sexual undertone
jokes.explanation: Troy's natural athleticism leading to unexpectedly intense coaching. The escalation works for his character.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Alright, listen up! You see this whistle? This whistle is my voice now. When I blow it, you MOVE. You feel me? I want you pushing, I want you sweating, I want you giving me 110% of that body!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Come on, get lower! Lower! Feel that burn? That's what I'm talking about. I need you hot, I need you tired, I need you completely exhausted by the end of this.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Don't you dare quit on me! You're going to take what I'm giving you and you're going to like it!

jokes.text: Release the whores.
jokes.setup: Dean has compromised his values to compete
jokes.punchline: Casual command treating sex workers like party entertainment
jokes.explanation: Shows Dean's complete moral compromise. Economical but relies on shock value over cleverness.

jokes.text: Well, if you like the place so much, why don't you gay marry it?
jokes.setup: Jeff compliments the barbershop
jokes.punchline: Pierce's outdated homophobic 'insult' used as genuine suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - outdated reference used earnestly. Character integration is excellent even if humor is dated.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: This barbershop is amazing. They really know what they're doing.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Yeah, well you should go down the street to the other one. Those guys? Total... you know... they're very good at their jobs and really care about precision grooming.

jokes.text: If you're so proud... Why don't you gay marry me?
jokes.setup: Pierce expresses pride in Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff turns Pierce's phrase back on him in touching moment
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that creates unexpected emotional moment. Jeff uses Pierce's language to show affection.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm proud of you, Jeff.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm proud of you too, Pierce.

jokes.text: You drew the short straw. Well, that's how it started, but I really did end up having a good time.
jokes.setup: Pierce realizes he's being managed
jokes.punchline: Jeff's honest admission with typical emotional reservation
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff moment showing growth - honest about manipulation but also genuine connection.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Wait, you're managing me?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yes. And honestly, it's exhausting.

jokes.text: I'm glad I didn't have kids. They just end up disappointing you.
jokes.setup: Pierce mentions regretting not having a son
jokes.punchline: Cynical reversal about children being disappointing
jokes.explanation: Dark Pierce wisdom that's both funny and sad. Reveals his fear of disappointment through cynicism.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I always regretted not having a son.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Then I realized... all my friends' sons are disappointments. So I basically got the experience without any of the effort.

jokes.text: I want that to be my thing now. I don't think you can do that.
jokes.setup: Archie decides to steal Magnitude's catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Annie's matter-of-fact rejection of the idea
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Archie's entitlement and Annie's practical nature.

dialogue.speaker: Archie
dialogue.line: I'm going to steal Magnitude's catchphrase. From now on, I'm saying 'Pop pop!'

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: No you're not.

jokes.text: Magnitude, you're not to say 'pop pop' ever again. Not pop? That's right. Find a new thing.
jokes.setup: Dean accommodates Archie's desire for catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Magnitude's confused clarification about just 'pop'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Dean's willingness to sacrifice school identity. Magnitude's confusion adds humor.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Magnitude, I understand you'd like your own catchphrase.

dialogue.speaker: Magnitude
dialogue.line: Pop pop!

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Yes, yes, very good. Pop pop.

dialogue.speaker: Magnitude
dialogue.line: No, no. Just pop.

jokes.text: Pop pop!
jokes.setup: Being told he can't say his catchphrase anymore
jokes.punchline: Magnitude's defiant continuation of his signature phrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. Magnitude's simple rebellion against corporate oppression is heroic.

dialogue.speaker: Magnitude
dialogue.line: Pop pop!

jokes.text: The only thing weighing on me is tons and tons of whale money.
jokes.setup: Annie asking if the ethics bother her
jokes.punchline: Britta's supposed mercenary attitude
jokes.explanation: Britta trying to be tough about money, which is against her character. The 'tons and tons' is appropriately over-the-top.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Does the ethics of it bother you?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Not even a little bit. I'm a mercenary.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You're not a mercenary.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Well, not a good one.

jokes.text: Don't talk about Pierce like that. Look, maybe the only reason he can be so unpleasant is because we expect him to be.
jokes.setup: Group complains about Pierce
jokes.punchline: Jeff's unexpected defense and psychological insight
jokes.explanation: Excellent character growth moment for Jeff. Shows emotional development while maintaining his analytical nature.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Look, I know Pierce can be difficult, but you're all being unfair. He's not trying to be offensive—he's just emotionally stunted from a lifetime of privilege and unexamined biases. Which, granted, makes him offensive. But the point is, he's not a bad guy. He's just a guy who's never had to think about why the things he says are problematic, so he doesn't. It's actually kind of sad when you think about it. Although, yes, still annoying.

jokes.text: Diggity doo? My God, what have we done?
jokes.setup: Magnitude tries new catchphrase alternatives
jokes.punchline: Annie's horror at witnessing the destruction of his identity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the consequences. Annie's dramatic reaction makes the absurd situation feel important.

dialogue.speaker: Magnitude
dialogue.line: Pop what, what, what?

dialogue.speaker: Magnitude
dialogue.line: Pop pop... skeet skeet?

dialogue.speaker: Magnitude
dialogue.line: Pop pop... yeet?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: No, no, no, no, no! Stop! You're going to destroy who you are!

jokes.text: I had to hitchhike home last night in a burrito truck. It's not as fun as it sounds.
jokes.setup: Annie explains why she's back in PE
jokes.punchline: Specific detail followed by deflating reality check
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail with realistic deflation. The economy is excellent - maximum humor per word.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I'm back in PE because I realized that physical fitness is just as important as academic excellence, and I wanted to set a good example for the study group by maintaining a holistic approach to health and wellness.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Plus, I heard they were serving those little cups of water at the end of class.

jokes.text: No one deserves to be mocked for being mock-locked in a mock locker.
jokes.setup: Annie apologizing for behavior in PE
jokes.punchline: Elaborate wordplay callback to mocker room concept
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay callback that extends the mocker room terminology

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I'm sorry for how I acted in PE class. I was completely out of line.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's okay, Annie. We all have our moments in the locker room.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Well, I guess you could say I really got dressed down for my behavior.

jokes.text: Greendale is a nice welcome banner. Greendale is a foamless cafeteria. And Greendale is Magnitude saying 'pop pop.'
jokes.setup: Dean realizes he's compromised school's identity
jokes.punchline: Lists specific humble but authentic elements
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dean's realization. The specific details make Greendale's humble identity touching.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: We have a modest library, humble athletics, and a cafeteria that serves food that is technically edible. These are the things that make Greendale authentic.

jokes.text: Everybody kisses my ass because my dad is loaded, and they always give me free stuff, like that jet ski I sunk, but it would be pretty sweet to be treated like a normal dude...
jokes.setup: Archie responds to Dean's authentic pitch
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of destroying expensive gift, desire for normalcy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Archie has depth. The jet ski detail reinforces his destructive wealth while showing self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Archie
dialogue.line: I appreciate the pitch, Dean, but I just want to be normal. My parents sent me a vintage motorcycle for my birthday, and I crashed it into a lake. I didn't even care. That's how much I want a normal life.

jokes.text: Delta Cubes! Delta Cubes! Delta Cubes! Delta Cubes! Delta Cubes! Delta Cubes!
jokes.setup: Happy ending to episode
jokes.punchline: Abed's triumphant return to fraternity character with excessive repetition
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Abed's commitment to his bits. The repetition creates comedic rhythm.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm back, baby! I'm back, baby! I'm back, baby!

jokes.text: Delta Cubes, operation, slack attack! Slack attack?
jokes.setup: Delta Cubes fraternity reappearing
jokes.punchline: Abed's confusion at his own made-up fraternity operation name
jokes.explanation: Good running gag payoff with Abed breaking character to question his own improvisation

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Wait, what? Delta Cubes? I made that up. Why would I make up a fraternity with a name that sounds like a frozen food brand?

jokes.text: You know what sport I never got? Golf. No argument here. They lost me immediately with the clown clothes.
jokes.setup: Pierce and Jeff bonding at barbershop
jokes.punchline: Shared opinion about golf attire being ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Nice bonding moment with shared observation. 'Clown clothes' is a good specific description.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know what's ridiculous? All these guys wearing those plaid pants to play golf.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Completely ridiculous. I mean, it's a sport, not a costume party.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Exactly! And those visors? Come on.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: The worst part is they think they look good.

jokes.text: Get your damn hand off of my Let's!
jokes.setup: Let's potato chips commercial plays
jokes.punchline: Troy aggressively defends his snack choice using commercial slogan
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Troy's susceptibility to advertising. Character-appropriate brand loyalty.

jokes.text: Honestly, after all that, you still eat splingles? Uh, yeah, Troy. Splingles have 30% less fat. They're the fit chip.
jokes.setup: Troy's aggressive brand loyalty to Let's
jokes.punchline: Reveals he actually eats competitor with health-conscious justification
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's contradictions. The health justification adds to the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So you're telling me you've been this aggressive about Let's, and you don't even eat it?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Well, I eat Competitor's brand. It has fewer calories and more natural ingredients.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You've been defending Let's this whole time while eating their competitor?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Health is important to me, Jeff.

jokes.text: Get your damn hand off of my Let's!
jokes.setup: End credits scene with Let's potato chips commercial
jokes.punchline: Dean's continued aggressive defense of fake product placement
jokes.explanation: Good callback that bookends the episode with Dean's product placement commitment

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Let's Potato Chips. The only chips endorsed by Greendale Community College.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: And yes, before you ask, I am being paid to say this. A LOT.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: So buy Let's Potato Chips, or I will personally track down every single one of you and make your lives miserable.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Let's Potato Chips. That's not a suggestion.

tmdb_overview: Dean Pelton enlists the study group to beautify the campus to woo a wealthy student to Greendale. A class on teaching P.E. pits Shirley against Troy.
insight: Season 4's weakest stretch: mid-tier humor with zero standout moments despite solid joke density.
